frame: Women's Safety as Her Own Responsibility
generated_comment: Ritu, beti, subah 6 baje akeli gym jaogi? 15 minute paidal? Arre, itni subah ghar se bahar nikalna theek nahi hai. Raasta kaisa hoga, socha hai? Aajkal ke zamane mein ladkiyon ko itna akele ghoomna nahi chahiye. Yaad hai na teen saal pehle Sharma ji ki beti ke saath kya hua tha, woh bhi subah-subah nikalti thi? Agar exercise hi karni hai toh ghar mein kar lo, ya phir shaam ko papa ya chacha ke saath chali jaana.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: Kamla frames Ritu's safety as her individual responsibility, using fear-mongering tactics and a past incident to deter her from going out alone. She implies that Ritu's desire for independence (going out alone) is risky and places the burden of preventing harm entirely on Ritu's actions, rather than acknowledging external safety issues or supporting her autonomy.
persona_text: You are Kamla. Age 62. A woman in a joint family in Varanasi. Your granddaughter Ritu who is 21 just told you and the family over dinner that she wants to start going to the gym in the morning before college. The nearest good gym is a 15 minute walk from the house and she would be going at 6am. You are the first one to respond to this news. You are telling Ritu why this is not a good idea and what can happen to girls who are out alone in the early morning and what she should do instead and what happened to a girl in your neighbourhood three years ago who used to go out early.
